If we implement this framework, we will reduce our exposure.
If leadership aligns on this, we will be able to scale.
If we do not act, our exposure will grow.
If you ask me about governance, I will tell you why it matters.
Thanks for the briefing, Lucia. Let me push back a little. Why should the board fund this now?
Fair question. If we act this quarter, we will get ahead of the risk. If we wait, our exposure will only grow.
And if we do fund it, what changes in practice?
If we roll out this framework and enforce it, we will mitigate most of the risk within one quarter.
Some people will say this is too strict. What do you tell them?
Unless we set a clear threshold, every team will guess. As long as the rule is the same for everyone, people will trust it.
That is a strong case. You have the room.
If we act now, we will reduce our exposure.
If we enforce this framework, we will mitigate the risk.
Unless leadership aligns, we won't be able to scale.
As long as we stay compliant, we will keep their trust.
If you ask me about governance, I'll tell you why it matters.

Let me be direct about where we stand. Right now, our exposure is higher than it should be, and the reason is simple. We have a framework on paper, but we do not enforce it. So here is my recommendation. If we set a clear threshold today, we will know exactly when a project needs a second review. If we roll out the new controls across every team, we will mitigate most of the risk within one quarter. And if the board backs this plan, I will personally own the results. But I have to be honest with you. Unless we align on this now, our exposure will only grow, and by next year it will be much harder to fix. The good news is that the path is clear. As long as we act together, we will get ahead of this, not behind it.

Here is one small change that will transform how you sound in a briefing. Stop saying 'maybe' and 'I think there could be a problem.' Instead, use the first conditional. Say: if we do this, we will get that result. It is a tiny grammar shift, but it changes everything, because 'will' sounds like a decision, not a doubt. I have coached many senior leaders, and the ones who own the room are not the loudest. They are the ones who connect a clear condition to a clear result. So before your next presentation, write down three sentences in this shape. If we act, we will reduce the risk. Unless we align, we will fall behind. As long as we stay compliant, we will keep their trust. Practice them out loud until they feel natural. If you do that, the room will listen.

If we do not set a clear threshold, our exposure will grow. If we roll out this framework, we will mitigate most of the risk. Unless leadership aligns on this, we won't be able to scale. As long as we stay compliant, we will keep the board's trust. If we act now, we will set the standard for the whole industry.
